I agree with the original conclusion. Thumb sucking is indeed a common and adaptive self-soothing behavior that typically resolves spontaneously by age 3–4, making it developmentally normal within the first three years of life; sustained use beyond this period is associated with increased risks of malocclusion due to prolonged pressure on developing teeth and jaws.
